events.text: Q1 FY26 print: revenue US$151.1M (+51% Y/Y, +13% Q/Q) — 4th consecutive record quarter, in line with the US$150–165M guide; non-GAAP GM 29.2%; non-GAAP loss/share US$(0.07). Data center US$81.4M (+154% Y/Y); 800G only US$4.6M (ramp still ahead); CATV US$66.8M (+24% Q/Q); cash US$449.4M; capex US$68.7M.

events.src: AAOI · Q1 FY26 earnings call (Thompson Lin)
events.text: FY26 raised to >US$1.1B revenue with >US$140M non-GAAP op income. Management explicit that FY26 is capacity-gated, not demand-gated — Thompson Lin put true demand at US$1.4–1.5B, internal target US$1.2B. By mid-2027, ~US$471M/month of DC transceiver revenue (~780k transceivers/month).

The 2H'26 ramp is a step function, not linear — Q3 and Q4 each +60–80% Q/Q as Texas capacity comes online. Capacity: ~150k/mo 800G+1.6T this quarter, >650k/mo by YE26 (~30% Texas), >930k/mo by YE27 (>50% Texas). 1.6T contributes late-2026 (Lin: "more than US$2B for 1.6T transceivers" next year).

Crux Capital/Gaetano: "$30B or $3B?" — "plenty of execution risk: capacity must expand dramatically + capex ramp + dilution on table + margins improve + new orders qualified + qualified orders ship on time".
@BenBajarin: the market needs to believe the 2027 ramp extends into a much larger base in 2028, not a one-year hyperscaler pull-forward.
Jim Cramer (CNBC): the optical names are "parabolic… not going to be good for the market".
ParadisLabs ranks AAOI behind LITE/COHR on order-book scale + commercial readiness. AAOI appeared on Rothschild's "12m relative highs" list.
Crux Capital/Gaetano: "the story is there, but we need the execution follow-through".

650 Group: AAOI named among the new entrants in the merchant laser market (alongside MACOM, Semtech) as the CPO laser opportunity scales from the low hundreds of millions to ~US$1B; the flip side is explicit oversupply/glut risk as everyone adds laser capacity.
Game-theory leg: AMD actively chasing CW laser supply (flagged in Rosenblatt checks) → once NVDA/AMD lock LTAs, AMZN/MSFT "must follow".

@aleabitoreddit (Serenity): ran from "$30 → $170/$175" and frames US$471M/month = ~US$5.6B ARR against a ~US$13.5B market cap in H1'27, "largest US transceiver supply chain for mass production of 800G/1.6T", with the bigger inflection in 2028.

cells: Confident vs soft MSO capex; new customers
